text: Traditional mixed training directed at many athletic abilities simultaneously produces conflicting physiological responses that limit adaptation in high-performance athletes.
Prolonged periods of multi-targeted training elicit excessive fatigue that undermines training quality and adaptation.
Mixed training with medium and low concentration workloads provides insufficient training stimulation to drive adaptation in high-performance athletes.
Traditional periodization is unable to support multi-peak performances across a competitive season.

Block periodization sequences specialized training cycles containing highly concentrated workloads directed at a minimal number of targeted abilities, enabling consecutive rather than simultaneous development.
Block periodization uses a taxonomy of mesocycle blocks (accumulation, transmutation, realization) as building units for compiling an annual training plan.
